# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Service configuration that exposes our application to the internet using a LoadBalancer. The app serves HTTP traffic on port 80 and HTTPS on port 443. Make it accessible from any source for maximum flexibility. This is for our public-facing API.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1 # Define the version of Kubernetes that this service configuration applies to (v1) in YAML format 
kind: Service # Defines a kind object, e.g., Deployment or Pod here it is representing as an 'Service'  
metadata:          # Metadata defined for your objects inside our cluster    
annotations:      # Annotate the service to use NodePort when you want public access (i.e., outside of K8s Network) 
kubernetes.io/service-name: api    # Name used by kubelet as a mount point in Pod's container filesystem, e.g 'api'. Required for DNS mapping and service discovery at load balancers with NodePorts (i.e., outside of K8s Network).
spec:              # Specification defining how the created object should behave 
type: LoadBalancer    # Type defined as a Service which exposes traffic, this is an external IP to access your application from any source or location inside our cluster  
ports:             # List out ports that are exposed by service     
- port: 80          # Outgoing requests on the specified port (i.e., HTTP)   
  name: http        # Name of a protocol which is used in accessing your application, e.g 'http'. Required for DNS mapping and routing purposes to an external load balancer with NodePorts  
- port: 443         # Outgoing requests on the specified SSL (HTTPS) Port(i., ie , HTTPS). This should be used only when you want your application secure from public exposure, e.g trusted and encrypted traffic to prevent man in middle attacks if there's no ingress rules for https or similar
  name: https     # Name of a protocol which is utilized while accessing the app (i., HTTPS). Required by Traefik as an SSL termination point inside K8s, it will redirect incoming requests on port and secure them using configured TLS settings. Optional if you have enabled service sidecar injection in your kubernetes with ingress-nginx controller which handles https for users
